title: Flatten a nested call into a named local
note: lifts each inner call out of its argument slot into a
, so every call reads on its own line. Simplicity is AST depth — one nested expression becomes two shallow statements.

title: Set an if-test slot to a new expression
note: sets an if-statement's test slot to a new expression — the condition
becomes
. The two-sided guard that refuses a statement (the paired rejection) accepts these: arg 1 is an if-statement, arg 2 an expression.

title: Remove an unused import
note: drops only imports no code references —
is imported but never called, so its line goes;
stays because
uses it. This is the remove-only third of what
does.

title: Canonicalize import paths
note: rewrites every import to its canonical repo path — here
becomes
— adding and removing nothing. It is the canonicalize third of
, shown alone.

title: A single-item helper refuses a two-item list
note: Exactly-one is an invariant, not a hope:
asserts its list has one item (via
) before returning it, so a two-item list is refused at the boundary — the same guard
leans on to require a single-export file.
expectText: throws — a single list has exactly one item

title: A slot setter refuses a statement in an expression slot
note: The if-test slot holds an expression; a statement in it is refused by the two-sided guard (
fails on arg 1).
expectText: throws — arg 1 is not an expression node

title: A frozen app is refused for prod build, promote, and delete
note: throws for any app on
, guarding every path that would change or remove its production assets. Non-frozen apps pass.
expectText: throws — replace is on the frozen list

title: Repair imports across a whole multi-function file
note: A migration file can hold many declarations; program-wide repair adds every missing repo import — single-export
would fail here. Locally-declared names (
) are never self-imported.

title: Auto-imports: add missing, remove unused, canonicalize paths
note: composes all three import verbs —
(used, unimported) is added,
(imported, unused) is removed,
(wrong
path) is canonicalized to
. Provably auto-safe: idempotent, total, state-independent.

title: Add a two-sided guard to a slot setter
note: prepends
and repairs imports; one predicate per parameter.

title: Generate a node-type predicate from scratch
note: creates a new
predicate;
is empty because nothing existed. Runs file-side (sandboxed) — see the app or the temp-file runner.
